stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including 'transformers' and 'llama'.
The pipeline indicates its primary function is text generation, utilizing safetensors format.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under the text-generation pipeline, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The associated tags (transformers, llama, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern AI/ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Dozens of Red Hat packages backdoored through its official NPM channel
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Dozens of Red Hat packages were found to be backdoored.
The compromise occurred through Red Hat's official NPM channel.
Users who downloaded the affected packages are urged to investigate immediately.

stories.title: Florida sues OpenAI, Sam Altman after multiple ChatGPT-linked murders
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Florida Attorney General has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman.
The lawsuit alleges that ChatGPT usage is linked to multiple murders.
The AG claims Altman demonstrates an 'utter disregard' for human lives.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It is associated with the deepseek_v3 architecture and utilizes standard ML formats like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including transformers and pytorch.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads on Macs.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The key feature is running all AI processes entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run tools like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex using local or cloud models.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being deployed in partnership with ROOST.
The models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: Gemini Spark is the most impressive and terrifying AI experience I’ve had yet
stories.author: David Pierce
bullet_points: The article focuses on Gemini Spark, positioning it as a highly impressive and potentially disruptive AI experience.
Trip planning is highlighted as a long-standing, yet often over-promised, killer use case for AI agents.
The piece suggests Gemini Spark may be a significant step forward in realizing the full potential of AI for complex tasks like travel planning.

stories.title: Hackers duped Meta AI support chatbot to steal celebrity Instagram accounts
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Hackers exploited a vulnerability in Meta AI support chatbots.
The exploit allowed unauthorized access to and theft of celebrity Instagram accounts.
The stolen, high-value Instagram handles were resold before Meta applied a patch.

stories.title: AdaCodec: A Predictive Visual Code for Video MLLMs
stories.author: Jiaqi Wang
bullet_points: Introduces AdaCodec, a predictive visual code designed to address redundancy in video data for MLLMs.
Current video MLLMs inefficiently encode every frame as an independent RGB image, leading to token repetition.
The proposed method suggests transmitting a full reference frame only when prediction fails, otherwise using a compact inter-frame description.

stories.title: SafeSteer: Localized On-Policy Distillation for Efficient Safety Alignment
stories.author: Lei Sha
bullet_points: Addresses the 'alignment tax' in LLMs, where safety fine-tuning degrades general capabilities.
Proposes SafeSteer, a method for localized on-policy distillation for efficient safety alignment.
Argues that safety features are sparse, necessitating localized modifications over global trade-offs.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run locally on the user's own hardware.
The framework includes built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM usage.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation functionality.
The feature is currently experimental and available on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code functionality within Ollama.
It facilitates the use of proprietary models like Claude alongside open-source models in the Ollama ecosystem.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Ableton is letting musicians build browser-style extensions for Live
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Ableton Live now supports a new Extensions SDK allowing users to build browser-style features.
This new SDK enables the use of common JavaScript to extend Ableton Live's functionality.
It expands capabilities beyond Max for Live, which is primarily focused on MIDI and audio processing.

stories.title: Impulse Space raises $500 million as orbital maneuvering race heats up
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: Impulse Space secured $500 million in funding.
The funding highlights an intensifying competition in orbital maneuvering technology.
The investment signals continued investor interest in emerging space technology sectors.

stories.title: AI costs how much? GitHub Copilot users react to new usage-based pricing system.
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: GitHub Copilot is implementing a new usage-based pricing model for its AI features.
Users are experiencing high consumption rates, with some exhausting their monthly credit allowance quickly.
The article focuses on user reactions to the shift in AI service monetization.

stories.title: Moderna gets $50 million to develop mRNA Ebola vaccine against Bundibugyo
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: Moderna has secured $50 million in funding.
The funding is earmarked for developing an mRNA vaccine.
The vaccine targets Ebola, specifically against the Bundibugyo strain, amidst an outbreak.

stories.title: Microsoft's Surface Laptop Ultra looks like its first true MacBook Pro competitor
stories.author: Andrew Cunningham
bullet_points: Microsoft's Surface Laptop Ultra is positioned as a serious competitor to the MacBook Pro.
The device is characterized as Microsoft's most refined effort in the high-end mobile workstation category.
The article suggests the laptop represents a significant step toward a less unconventional Microsoft premium offering.

stories.title: Mitigating Perceptual Judgment Bias in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge via Perceptual Perturbation and Reward Modeling
stories.author: Hyunjung Shim
bullet_points: Identifies 'Perceptual Judgment Bias' in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge systems.
This bias causes MLLMs to favor plausible textual narratives over perceptually accurate visual evidence.
Proposes using perceptual perturbation and reward modeling to mitigate this evaluation weakness.

stories.title: ClinEnv: An Interactive Multi-Stage Long Horizon EHR Environment for Agents
stories.author: May Dongmei Wang
bullet_points: Introduces ClinEnv, a new interactive benchmark designed to test LLMs in complex clinical decision-making.
Addresses the limitation of static benchmarks by simulating real, sequential, and irreversible decisions during inpatient admissions.
Aims to evaluate LLMs as attending physicians by handling heterogeneous information incrementally under uncertainty.

stories.title: Permissive Safety Through Trusted Inference: Verifiable Belief-Space Neural Safety Filters for Assured Interactive Robotics
stories.author: Haimin Hu
bullet_points: The paper proposes 'Verifiable Belief-Space Neural Safety Filters' for autonomous robots.
These filters aim to ensure safety in interactive robotics when facing human uncertainty (preferences, goals, etc.).
The approach maintains the modularity of traditional safety filters while extending their scope beyond purely physical constraints.

stories.title: From Layers to Submodules: Rethinking Granularity in Replacement-Based LLM Compression
stories.author: Giovanni Iacca
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in current LLM compression techniques, specifically those relying on full-layer granularity and contiguous component selection.
It proposes rethinking the granularity of replacement-based compression by moving from entire layers to smaller submodules.
The research suggests that redundancy in pre-trained transformers is not strictly confined to contiguous regions, opening new avenues for more precise model pruning.

stories.title: Modeling Depth Ambiguity: A Mixture-Density Representation for Flying-Point-Free Depth Estimation
stories.author: Jun Gao
bullet_points: Addresses the 'flying-point' failure mode in depth estimation, where spurious 3D points appear near object boundaries.
Identifies the root cause as the limitation of standard models that assign a single depth hypothesis per pixel.
Proposes a Mixture-Density Representation to model the inherent depth ambiguity at object boundaries.

stories.title: SimSD: Simple Speculative Decoding in Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Jingbo Shang
bullet_points: Introduces SimSD, a method to apply speculative decoding to Diffusion Language Models (dLLMs).
Addresses a key limitation: dLLMs' masked language modeling formulation is incompatible with standard AR speculative decoding.
Highlights the potential of dLLMs as a faster inference alternative to traditional autoregressive (AR) LLMs.

stories.title: ProtoAda: Prototype-Guided Adaptive Adapter Expansion and Geometric Consolidation for Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning
stories.author: Da-Wei Zhou
bullet_points: The paper addresses Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning (MCIT) for Large Language Models (MLLMs) to allow them to acquire new vision-language capabilities over time.
It proposes 'ProtoAda,' a method utilizing Prototype-Guided Adaptive Adapter Expansion and Geometric Consolidation to manage continual learning.
The research aims to mitigate inter-task interference, a common challenge in continual learning, especially when tasks have distinct response structures.

stories.title: Permissive Safety Through Trusted Inference: Verifiable Belief-Space Neural Safety Filters for Assured Interactive Robotics
stories.author: Haimin Hu
bullet_points: The research addresses safety in interactive robotics where robots must handle human uncertainty (preferences, goals, etc.).
It proposes 'Verifiable Belief-Space Neural Safety Filters' to ensure safety while maintaining task efficiency.
This approach builds upon existing safety filter concepts but aims to extend their operation beyond purely physical space.

stories.title: Auditing Asset-Specific Preferences in Financial Large Language Models: Evidence from Bitcoin Representations and Portfolio Allocation
stories.author: Wenbin Wu
bullet_points: Investigates potential asset-specific biases within Large Language Models (LLMs) used in financial applications like robo-advisors.
Proposes a three-level audit protocol to test if LLMs systematically prefer certain financial instruments, using Bitcoin as a case study.
Aims to identify internal representations within LLMs that causally influence asset preferences and subsequent financial decisions.

stories.title: Drifting Preference Optimization for One-Step Generative Models
stories.author: Zhen Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the difficulty of preference finetuning for one-step text-to-image generators, which are desirable for deployment due to single-pass generation.
Introduces Drifting Preference Optimization (DrPO), a novel online preference-finetuning method specifically designed for deterministic one-step generators.
Offers an alternative to standard alignment methods that typically rely on complex mechanisms like policy likelihoods or differentiable reward gradients.

stories.title: Towards Automated Discovery: A Review of Generative Models, Multimodal Learning and Closed-Loop Workflows in Inverse Materials Design
stories.author: Gian-Marco Rignanese
bullet_points: The paper reviews the shift in materials discovery from forward prediction to targeted candidate proposal using inverse materials design.
It surveys recent advancements in generative crystal structure modeling and multimodal learning for crystalline solids.
The review focuses on closed-loop design pipelines, detailing how modern generators learn chemical-structural priors for controllable structure sampling.

stories.title: Iteris: Agentic Research Loops for Computational Mathematics
stories.author: Bin Dong
bullet_points: Introduces 'Iteris,' an agentic research system designed to advance computational mathematics.
Addresses the gap where current LLM/AI research often overlooks the complex needs of mathematical discovery (proofs, numerical experiments, algorithm design).
Leverages recent advancements in agentic AI to tackle research-level conjectures in mathematics.

stories.title: AdaCodec: A Predictive Visual Code for Video MLLMs
stories.author: Jiaqi Wang
bullet_points: Introduces AdaCodec, a predictive visual coding method designed for video MLLMs.
Addresses the inefficiency of current video MLLMs that treat each frame as an independent RGB image, leading to redundant token encoding.
Proposes transmitting only a full reference frame when prediction fails, otherwise sending a compact description of inter-frame changes.

stories.title: ClinEnv: An Interactive Multi-Stage Long Horizon EHR Environment for Agents
stories.author: May Dongmei Wang
bullet_points: Introduces ClinEnv, a new interactive benchmark designed to test Large Language Models (LLMs) in clinical settings.
Addresses the limitation of static benchmarks by simulating real, sequential, and uncertain decision-making processes found in inpatient admissions.
Aims to evaluate LLMs as attending physicians by modeling longitudinal, multi-stage clinical interactions.

stories.title: From Layers to Submodules: Rethinking Granularity in Replacement-Based LLM Compression
stories.author: Giovanni Iacca
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in current LLM compression techniques, which rely on full-layer granularity and contiguous component selection.
It proposes rethinking the granularity of replacement-based compression, suggesting that redundancy in transformers is not limited to contiguous blocks.
The research implies that compression strategies should account for uneven redundancy distribution between Attention and FeedForward network outputs.

stories.title: HERO'S JOURNEY: Testing Complex Rule Induction with Text Games
stories.author: Junyi Jessy Li
bullet_points: Introduces HERO'S JOURNEY, a new benchmark specifically designed for testing complex rule induction in goal-directed episodic tasks.
The benchmark tests agents' ability to infer hidden rules from demonstrations across various attribute and procedural induction families.
Evaluations on state-of-the-art LLMs show evidence of rule induction, but current capabilities are noted as being limited.

stories.title: SN-WER: Script-Normalized WER for Multi-Script Indic ASR Evaluation
stories.author: Priyaranjan Pattnayak
bullet_points: Introduces SN-WER, a new metric to address inaccuracies in Word Error Rate (WER) for Indic ASR.
SN-WER solves the problem where WER overestimates errors due to script differences between reference and hypothesis.
The method is training-free and evaluation-only, involving transliteration into a canonical script before scoring.

stories.title: Transferable Self-Harm Surveillance from Emergency Department Triage Notes Using an Evidence-Augmented Machine Learning Approach
stories.author: Vlada Rozova
bullet_points: The research addresses the public health challenge of self-harm surveillance, noting limitations in current diagnostic coding.
It proposes using Emergency Department (ED) triage notes as a more sensitive data source for early detection.
The methodology combines traditional machine learning with Large Language Model (LLM)-based screening and evidence extraction.

stories.title: SimSD: Simple Speculative Decoding in Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Jingbo Shang
bullet_points: Introduces SimSD, a method to apply speculative decoding to Diffusion Language Models (dLLMs).
Addresses a key limitation: the masked language modeling formulation of dLLMs is incompatible with standard AR speculative decoding.
Highlights the potential of dLLMs as a faster inference alternative to traditional autoregressive (AR) LLMs.

stories.title: SkillHarm: Lifecycle-Aware Skill-Based Attacks via Automated Construction
stories.author: Huan Sun
bullet_points: Introduces SkillHarm, a new benchmark for skill-based attacks targeting AI agents.
Addresses the vulnerability of third-party skills within agent workflows, which are often implicitly executed.
Moves beyond single-task evaluations by analyzing attacks across the entire skill-use lifecycle.

stories.title: SafeSteer: Localized On-Policy Distillation for Efficient Safety Alignment
stories.author: Lei Sha
bullet_points: Addresses the 'alignment tax' in LLMs, where safety alignment degrades general capabilities.
Proposes SafeSteer, a method for localized on-policy distillation for efficient safety alignment.
Argues that safety features are sparse, necessitating localized modifications over global trade-offs.

stories.title: FigSIM: A Dataset for Fine-grained Suicide Severity and Figurative Language in Suicide Memes
stories.author: Mike Conway
bullet_points: The research introduces FigSIM, a new dataset specifically designed for analyzing suicide memes.
The study addresses the critical need to understand the characteristics of suicide memes for better content moderation on social media.
The dataset focuses on fine-grained analysis, including suicide severity and figurative language within these memes.

stories.title: Meta repeatedly snubs EU body over Facebook and Instagram user bans
stories.author: dijksterhuis
bullet_points: Meta is reportedly ignoring directives from an EU regulatory body.
The dispute centers on user bans related to Facebook and Instagram.
This highlights ongoing regulatory friction between major tech companies and EU governance.

stories.title: The newest Instagram “exploit” is the goofiest I've seen
stories.author: ssiddharth
bullet_points: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support bot to compromise Instagram accounts.
The exploit method is described as being relatively 'goofy' by the source.
This highlights security vulnerabilities within large-scale AI customer support systems.

stories.title: How fast is LlamaStash? Overhead, throughput, and a fair comparison with Ollama and LM Studio
stories.author: deepu105
bullet_points: The article provides a reproducible benchmark comparing LlamaStash against established LLM serving tools (Ollama, LM Studio, llama-server).
It evaluates performance metrics including overhead and throughput across diverse hardware configurations (AMD APU, Apple Silicon, NVIDIA).
The content is relevant to the practical deployment and optimization of local Large Language Models (LLMs).

stories.title: Introducing LlamaStash: a zero-overhead, terminal-native llama.cpp launcher
stories.author: deepu105
bullet_points: LlamaStash is a new tool built in Rust designed to launch local LLMs using llama.cpp.
It offers a unified interface including TUI, CLI, and daemon capabilities.
The tool functions as an OpenAI-compatible proxy, facilitating local LLM usage.

stories.title: Your AI Agent Isn't Failing Because It Hallucinates — It's Failing Because of Rate Limits
stories.author: p0rt
bullet_points: The primary production failure mode for LLM agents in 2026 is identified as capacity constraints, not reasoning errors or hallucinations.
The article suggests that current demonstrations often fail to reflect real-world load challenges.
It proposes specific capacity-engineering patterns necessary to maintain LLM agent stability under high load.
